response.reasoning: Step 1: Minimum qualifications — the candidate holds a BS in Computer Science and has roughly 17-20 years of professional software experience (freelance since 2006, full-time roles since 2009), so the 15-year bar and degree requirement are met. Step 2: Seniority match — the role is a Director-level principal IC focused on AI strategy; the candidate's trajectory is Senior/Staff engineer with hands-on delivery, not organization-level technical strategy, advising strategic customers, or driving disruptive initiatives at scale. Step 3: AI depth — recent work at re:cinq includes AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic-workflow tooling, which is relevant but recent (~1 year) and not 'recognized expertise in AI with demonstrated products or platforms.' No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field. Step 4: Entrepreneurial signal — founder of GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform, sustained since 2016) and early-startup experience (WatchUp) satisfy the entrepreneurial preference. Step 5: Cloud/builder fluency — strong polyglot builder (TypeScript, DLang, C/C++, high-throughput microservices at Dunnhumby, AWS CDK, Kubernetes), plus community talks and workshops showing some knowledge-sharing inclination. Step 6: Net assessment — an excellent hands-on senior/staff engineer who meets the minimum bar but falls short of the principal/director scope and the recognized-AI-expert profile this role centers on.
strengths: 17+ years of broad engineering experience across web, embedded, low-latency, and cloud systems
Genuine entrepreneurial track record as founder of a sustained open-source geo-data platform (GISCollective)
Recent hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work (MCP code generators, agentic workflow tooling)
concerns: No principal/director-level scope: career capped at senior/staff IC without evidence of org-wide technical strategy
AI experience is recent and tooling-focused, not the recognized AI expertise or shipped AI products the role prefers
No advanced degree in an AI-related field and limited evidence of advising executives or strategic customers

key_factors.factor: Seniority gap vs Director-level IC role
Meets 15-year experience minimum with entrepreneurial background

response.justification: The candidate clears the minimum qualifications (BS in CS, 15+ years) and brings strong builder fluency, startup/founder experience, and emerging AI-agent tooling work. However, the role demands a recognized AI expert operating at Director level — shaping cloud/AI product strategy, advising strategic customers, and inspiring at organizational scale — and the resume shows a hands-on senior/staff engineer without that strategic scope or established AI credentials. A capable engineer, but a level and specialization mismatch for this particular position.
